rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining. Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 6 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for a Water Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

During this turn, your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). During your turn, if this Grant is in your discard pile, you may discard 2 cards, except any Grant, from your hand. If you do, put this Grant into your hand. (This effect doesn't use up your Supporter card for the turn.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put 3 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put 1 of your Colorless Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all attached cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you discard 2 Metal Energy cards from your hand. Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

flavorText: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.
It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.
It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it's been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.
When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.
It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.
Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy— it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

attacks.name: Dyna Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.
On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.
Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it's being controlled by some mysterious being.
It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.
When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.
Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

attacks.name: Photon Boost
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

subtype: Basic, Fusion Strike, Single Strike, Rapid Strike
flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.
Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.
It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with what sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

attacks.name: Explosive Bolt
attacks.effect: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Current
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

attacks.name: Line Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.
It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Freeze
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Glistening Droplets
attacks.effect: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

attacks.name: Boltsplosion
attacks.effect: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

attacks.name: Cheerful Charge
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

rules:

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

flavorText: Professor Rowan

rules:
Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card into your hand. If you don't, discard that card and draw a card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

The Pokémon V this card is attached to can use the VSTAR Power on this card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Put up to 2 Pokémon, each with 90 HP or less, from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put up to 4 in any combination of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone. Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, look at the bottom 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. If tails, look at the bottom 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched Fighting Pokémon in any way you like.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

attacks.name: Rolling Rollout

flavorText: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

flavorText: With waves of its fan—made from leaves and its own fur—Oranguru skillfully gives instructions to other Pokémon.

flavorText: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!
Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

flavorText: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it's a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

flavorText: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It's accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.
It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

attacks.name: Counter Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

attacks.name: Wreak Havoc
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Bulky pincers account for one third of Scizor's body weight. A single swing of one of these pincers will crush a boulder completely.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

attacks.name: Assault Gate
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon didn't move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

attacks.name: Rocket Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If this Pokémon evolved from Skrelp during this turn, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1 during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.
It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It buries some of its prey in the sand to use as emergency meals when its hunts are unsuccessful.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

attacks.name: Lost Claw
attacks.effect: Put a random card from your opponent's hand in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.

attacks.name: Midnight Fang
attacks.effect: This attack does 80 less damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It charges headlong at its opponents, attacking without any care about what injuries it might receive.

attacks.name: Invite Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.
It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master's actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.
It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.
It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.
When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.
It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loving Sympathy
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

attacks.name: G-Max Smite
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Horoscope
attacks.effect: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Put the other cards back in any order.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.

attacks.name: Fade to Black
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.

attacks.name: Energy Mix
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Leap
attacks.effect: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psypump
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.effect: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor's three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don't seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

attacks.name: Crushing Beat
attacks.effect: You may discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Lightning Storm Star
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon 4 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 60 damage to it. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Reactive Pulse
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.

attacks.name: Max Fist
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.
When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.
These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That's why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

attacks.name: Scrap Pulse
attacks.effect: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Scrap Short
attacks.effect: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Seeking Fang
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

attacks.name: Electrostep
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scar Strikes
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

attacks.name: Shorting Spark
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool attached. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio's electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

attacks.name: Jumping Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.
All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

attacks.name: Frost Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Typhoon
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Aqua Typhoon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wave Summoning
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Dynamic Wave
attacks.effect: Put 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

attacks.name: Emotional Draw
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.
It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as -150 degrees Fahrenheit.
No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

attacks.name: Find It
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It'll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.
Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit's poisonous gas.

attacks.name: Hydro Burn
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it's trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold.
The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun.

attacks.name: Fireball Fever
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 5 cards from the top of your deck. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Ember Star
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your discard pile. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Power
attacks.effect: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Flare Juggling
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Explosive Fire
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Star Blaze
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: King's Instructions
attacks.effect: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bloomshine
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Triple Whip
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.effect: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Festering Saliva
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.
The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jet Wing
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

attacks.name: Swoop Across
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.
It lives by drinking only dewdrops from under the leaves of plants. It is said that it eats nothing else.

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As Scyther fights more and more battles, its scythes become sharper and sharper. With a single slice, Scyther can fell a massive tree.

attacks.name: Suctioning Vines
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.
Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.name: Flower Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

attacks.name: Offensive Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

attacks.name: Leaf Boomerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.
